issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-19.17-29-47.mp4

Overview

Migrate GoThru Photo Studio from Electron to Tauri v2 + Rust. Epic tracking the
phased migration. Fresh install — no migration of old user data (DB/settings start empty;
no NeDB importer).

Goal: smaller bundle, lower idle memory, tighter security (no full Node runtime in the renderer).

Strategy (current — supersedes the original "sidecars + Node bridge" plan)

Direct port to native Rust, feature-by-feature. The renderer is carried over as-is
(React 18 + TS strict, Tailwind v4 + HeroUI, Redux Toolkit, i18next, react-toastify);
the only renderer change is the bridge: window.electron.* → Tauri invoke/listen
(centralized in src/lib/electron-bridge.ts).

The Node sidecar is skipped by default — light channels are ported straight to Rust.
A sidecar / bundled external binary is used only for genuinely heavy channels
(360 binaries, exiftool write, AI image generation). This is a planned deviation from the
original Strategy A, kept consistent with the bundle/memory/security goal.

  • UX parity is mandatory (flow + output identical to Electron).
  • Code flow is free to improve — the Electron code is reference for what, not how
    (it has real bugs/anti-patterns; do not port those).

Migration surface (verified)

~168 IPC channels (142 registrations; the message dispatcher expands to 24 sub-types).
Backend split by phase target: core (Tauri plugins) ~15 · rust (port required) ~85 ·
sidecar (external binary / AI) ~40.

Phases

  • P0 — Inventory & docs — IPC inventory, per-feature docs, architecture map, stack/skills.
  • P1 — Scaffold — Tauri v2 shell; renderer moved to src/renderer; bridge window.electron.*
    → invoke/listen; Rust backend + plugins registered; store → RTK; app boots & UI renders.
  • P2 — Node sidecar — intentionally skipped (replaced by direct-to-Rust). Sidecar/bundled
    binary added only per heavy channel as needed.
  • P3 — Port to Rust, per feature — all features ported to native Rust (see checklist).
  • P4 — Cutover & cleanup — retire Electron, finalize updater/signing/packaging (Win + mac).
  • Final pass — UI polish on HeroUI (visual/a11y) once function + UX are solid.

✅ Features completed via AI

"AI" = ported/implemented by the assistant. Manual test boxes are left unchecked —
@esa08 will verify and tick these.

Feature AI ported Manual test
App shell (updater, first-run, deep-link, native menu, theme, lifecycle) ✅ [ ]
Auth — Google OAuth (login, account/log manager, API-key checks) ✅ ✅
Preference / Settings (all setters, store + SQLite) ✅ ✅
File Manager (list/watch, create/rename/move/copy/delete, dialogs, drag) ✅ ✅
Path / OS / System (home dir, special paths, clipboard, online, devtools) ✅ ✅
Preview / 360 Viewer (thumbnails, image view, RAW preview, 360 video detect) ✅ ✅
Metadata / EXIF / GPS (read EXIF, GPS read/write, dimensions, reverse-geocode) ✅ [ ]
Map (GPS markers, satellite tile source, custom URI scheme) ✅ [ ]
Cache (clear/clear-all, info, total size, prune unused thumbs) ✅ ✅
Favorites & Bookmarks (SQLite) ✅ [ ]
Upload / Publish (GoThru, Panorra, Google Street View) ✅ ✅
Edit › Rotate image (single + batch) ✅ ✅
Edit › Image straightener / equiRotate ✅ ✅
Edit › Image Resize (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › Minify JPG (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › Convert 360 (equirect ↔ cube faces, incl. face preview) ✅ ✅
Edit › Enfuse (HDR merge) ✅ ✅
Edit › Nadir Logo Patching & Builder ✅ ✅
Edit › Auto Level (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › AI Nadir Patching (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › Room Staging (AI) ✅ ✅
Edit › Virtual Staging 2D (AI) ✅ ✅
Edit › Eqroll (eqroll / eqroll-multiple) ✅ ✅
Edit › Eq2photo capture ✅ ✅
Edit › Scale Up Image ✅ ✅

Acceptance / cutover criteria

  1. All IPC channels have a verified Tauri/Rust (or justified sidecar) equivalent.
  2. UX + output parity confirmed vs Electron (viewer, map, every Edit tool).
  3. Updater + signing + notarization working on Win + mac.
  4. Installer size + idle memory measurably better than Electron.

Results — why the Tauri build is better

Installer / build size

  • Electron: ~300 MB — every Electron app bundles a full Chromium + Node.js runtime.
  • Tauri: ~80 MB — uses the OS-native webview (WebView2 on Windows, WKWebView on macOS) instead of shipping its own browser.
  • Still shrinkable in upcoming builds:
    • Rust release-profile optimization (strip, opt-level = "z", lto, panic = "abort"),
    • trim sidecar binaries to per-platform only (e.g. exiftool),
    • drop unused dependencies and optimize large assets (images/SVG),
    • code-split the renderer bundle.

Other gains

  • Lower idle memory — no bundled Chromium + Node processes running behind the UI.
  • Rust backend — heavy ops (directory listing, thumbnail decode/cache, rotate, 360 convert) are native: faster, memory-safe, with controlled parallelism (prewarm + semaphore) instead of a single Node event loop.
  • Tighter security — the renderer has no direct Node/fs access; everything goes through invoke/events gated by explicit Tauri capabilities. Much smaller attack surface than Electron's Node-in-renderer model.
  • Correct native integration per OS — native menu on macOS (global menu bar); HTML menu on Windows to avoid the WebView2 viewport-reflow flicker the native menu caused; native dialogs/clipboard/deep-link/notifications via Tauri plugins.
  • Lighter distribution & updates — a much smaller download for first install and auto-update.

Directly advances the issue Goal (smaller bundle, lower idle memory, tighter security) and acceptance criterion #4 (installer size + idle memory measurably better than Electron).
